source: multi-source web research (PlayStation Store, Xbox Store, psprices.com, gg.deals, xbox-store-checker.com, uflgame.com, dotesports, gamerant, startselect, gameland.gg, thegamer.com, deltiasgaming.com)
monetisation_philosophy: Skill-first approach, zero pay-to-win (developer claim). CEO stated 'players' success should not depend on the number of in-game purchases or the value of donations they make'. Community disputes this claim significantly.
payment_processor: Xsolla (Merchant of Record for web shop), platform stores (PlayStation Store, Xbox Store, Steam) for platform purchases

currency_system.note: UFL has four in-game currencies, two purchasable with real money and two earned through gameplay only.

currencies.name: Credit Points
currencies.description: Primary soft currency for everyday use. Used to buy base players from the transfer market, purchase basic packs (CP packs cost 49M CP each), customise club appearance with kits/boots/balls/gloves/badges/stadiums, boost player recovery, and general enhancements.

currencies.name: League Points
currencies.description: Premium hard currency. Used to purchase Team Pass / Elite Pass, Player Card Skin Packs (pre-Season 25-26), premium packs (Mythic Packs etc. post-Season 25-26), customisation items (unique kits, stadium upgrades), XP/CP/RP Boosters, and extra player slots.

currencies.name: Reputation Points
currencies.description: Balancing mechanism that limits access to top-tier players. Acts as a squad cap - higher-rated players cost more RP. Cannot be bought, only earned through playing matches. Prevents pay-to-win by gating roster quality behind gameplay time.

currencies.name: Star Points
currencies.description: Tied to the Team Pass / Elite Pass (battle pass equivalent). Used to unlock rewards within the pass tiers including cosmetics, CP, LP, packs, and legends.

currencies.name: Particles
currencies.description: Introduced in Season 25-26. Obtained from discarding Mastery players and older skins. Used to open Particle packs.

lp_purchase_tiers.note: LP (League Points) is the premium hard currency. Prices sourced from multiple stores. USD prices are from PlayStation Store US and Xbox Store US. GBP prices from PlayStation Store UK. EUR price confirmed for 9500 LP only. Xbox Store Checker showed slightly different prices (possibly regional/converted); official US store prices listed as primary.

tiers.source_gbp: PlayStation Store UK via search results

tiers.note: Xbox search results showed $8.99 which may be a different region or sale price
tiers.source_gbp: PlayStation Store UK via search results

tiers.note: GBP price not confirmed independently; estimated ~£15.99 based on LP/GBP ratio from other tiers
GBP price not confirmed; estimated ~£32.99 based on ratio
Was discounted to $59.99 at one point. GBP price estimated ~£64.99 based on typical PSN conversion

lp_per_dollar_analysis.note: Value improves with higher tiers (standard volume discount pattern)

cp_purchase_tiers.note: CP (Credit Points) is the soft currency. Can be purchased but is also earnable through gameplay. Exact USD prices harder to confirm than LP prices; some sourced from gg.deals/xbox-store-checker which may show regional variants. Tiers confirmed via PlayStation Store product pages.

tiers.note: Price from xbox-store-checker ($0.85 shown, likely regional); US store estimated at $0.99 based on pattern
xbox-store-checker showed $3.41 (likely regional); US price estimated at $3.99 based on pricing curve
gg.deals showed historical low of $8.99; US price likely $9.99. GBP confirmed from UK PSN search results
tiers.source_gbp: PlayStation Store UK via search results

tiers.note: xbox-store-checker showed $37.37 (likely regional); US price estimated at $39.99 based on pricing curve

cp_per_dollar_analysis.note: CP value per dollar improves significantly at higher tiers

starter_packs.note: One-time purchase bundles designed for new players. Prices confirmed from PlayStation Store US and key resellers. These are the legacy packs from initial launch; new bundles (Starter Bundle, Special Bundle, Ultimate Bundle) were introduced in Season 25-26.

season_25_26_bundles.name: Starter Bundle
season_25_26_bundles.note: USD price not confirmed; GBP from PlayStation Store UK

value_packs_bundles.note: Combined currency packs that offer LP + CP together at a bundled price. These represent the higher-spend options.

packs.name: Extra Pack
packs.note: Buying LP and CP separately would cost ~$29.98 ($19.99 + $9.99), so bundle saves ~$4

packs.name: Grand Pack
packs.note: Buying separately would cost ~$119.98 ($79.99 + $39.99). Premium combined offer.

packs.name: Welcome Bundle
packs.contents: Unknown - likely a small introductory offer
packs.note: xbox-store-checker showed $2.24 (regional). Contents not confirmed from search results.

packs.name: Performance Pack
packs.note: CDKeysforgames showed PS5 key at $15.28. Contents not confirmed.

packs.name: Elite Bundle
packs.note: xbox-store-checker showed $60.52 (regional). Contents confirmed from search results.

themed_edition_packs.note: Limited-time or promotional bundles tied to real players or events

packs.name: Valverde / Raphinha Edition
packs.contents: Likely similar structure with both player cards + LP

packs.name: Tricky Leprechaun Pack
packs.contents: Unknown - seasonal St Patrick's Day pack

contents.note: Designed to help complete Team Pass; 1500 stars + bonus

packs.name: Bonus Pack

contents.boosters: CP, XP, and RP Boosters (30 matches)

packs.note: Exclusive PlayStation Plus benefit (free with PS Plus subscription)

contents.note: Very high chance for Mastery 3 cards (91-94 rated)
Very high chance for Mastery 3 cards (91-94 rated). Described as best-value offer.

team_pass_battle_pass.note: UFL's equivalent of a battle pass / season pass. Two tiers available.

tiers.name: Elite Pass
tiers.rewards: All Team Pass rewards + additional premium rewards + 700 SP total (200 base + 500 bonus)
tiers.upgrade_note: Can upgrade from Team to Elite at reduced cost (difference in LP)

team_pass_battle_pass.pass_content: Monthly rotation - each Team Pass brings a new Legend and themed items (e.g. Adidas Throwback Teampass with retro items). Rewards include cosmetics, CP, LP, packs, and Legend player cards (e.g. Del Piero).

in_game_store_items.note: Items purchasable within the game using LP or CP. Skin pack pricing is from the pre-Season 25-26 system; the Season 25-26 update replaced skins with a new Mastery card system.

skin_packs_legacy.name: Basic Skin Pack
skin_packs_legacy.note: Only pack purchasable with CP (soft currency). Exact CP cost not confirmed.

other_store_items.note: Post-Season 25-26 update shifted from skin system to pack-based card acquisition (Basic, Mastery M1/M2/M3, Legendary)

season_25_26_economy_changes.note: Major economy overhaul launched September 12, 2025. Significant shift in monetisation model.
changes: Removed old 'Skins' upgrade system (players acquired at fixed prices, upgraded through skins + XP)
Introduced pack-based card system: Basic, Mastery (M1, M2, M3), and Legendary tiers
Every card now has fixed attributes - squad progression through acquiring new cards, not upgrading old ones
New 'Link Up Play' chemistry system (nationality, country of play, special link-up type)
Introduced Particles currency (from discarding cards) for Particle packs
Monthly Team Pass rotation with new Legends
Weekly and monthly content updates tied to real-life football events
Player ratings now updated based on real-life performances

platforms.name: PlayStation Store
platforms.note: Full range of LP packs, CP packs, starter packs, and themed bundles

platforms.name: Xbox Store / Microsoft Store
platforms.note: Full range of LP packs, CP packs, starter packs, and themed bundles

platforms.name: Steam
platforms.note: Zero Steam DLC listed - all purchases are in-game only via Xsolla

platforms.name: UFL Web Shop (Xsolla)
platforms.note: Web-based store powered by Xsolla. Supports 1000+ payment methods. Final price/currency varies by payment method. No companion app identified.

purchase_channels.companion_app: No companion app found. The web shop at webshop.uflgame.com serves as the web-based purchase channel outside platform stores.

community_sentiment.note: Despite 'fair-to-play' branding, community reception of monetisation is mixed to negative.
key_complaints: Pay-to-win concerns: spending real money on CP gives significant advantage in acquiring top players faster
RP system acts as a time-gate but doesn't fully prevent pay-to-win since CP for players can be bought
Predatory mechanics cited: RP loss, bad matchmaking, pack odds
Hundreds of millions of CP needed for best players - either grind hundreds of matches or pay
Even with spending, RP gate limits how quickly you can build top squad
positive_notes: RP cannot be purchased - provides genuine time-gate against pure spending
Cosmetics are genuinely cosmetic (kits, badges, stadiums)
Free-to-play model means no upfront cost
Season 25-26 update attempted to make progression more transparent

data_gaps.note: The prices listed are best estimates based on multiple sources and pricing curve analysis

data_gaps.reason: Web shop uses Xsolla; prices vary by payment method and region. Could not scrape due to tool restrictions.
data_gaps.workaround: Web shop prices likely match or slightly undercut platform store prices (Xsolla takes lower cut than platform 30%)

data_gaps.gap: Contents and pricing for Welcome Bundle, Performance Pack, Champion Bundle
data_gaps.reason: These appeared in store listings but contents/prices not fully detailed in search results

data_gaps.gap: Pricing for Mythic Packs and other Season 25-26 pack types purchased with LP
data_gaps.reason: New pack system introduced but individual pack LP costs not surfaced in search results

data_gaps.gap: Historical pricing changes / whether prices changed at Season 25-26 launch
data_gaps.reason: No specific reporting on price changes found. LP/CP tier prices appear stable since launch.
Steam has zero DLC listings. All purchases happen through Xsolla in-game store. Pricing may differ from console stores.

key_insights: UFL uses a dual-currency model (CP soft + LP hard) which is industry standard for F2P games
LP price range ($0.99 to $79.99) mirrors EA FC Points range almost exactly - clearly benchmarked against EA
Team Pass at 1000 LP (~$9.99) is comparable to other F2P battle passes
RP system as a non-purchasable progression gate is a differentiator vs EA FC's fully monetised model
Web shop via Xsolla provides a platform-independent purchase channel (relevant for Goals if considering web store)
Season 25-26 shift from skins to card packs brought UFL closer to EA FC's Ultimate Team model
The 'fair-to-play' branding resonates with some players even if execution is debated - positioning lever for Goals
No companion app found - opportunity gap if Goals builds one
